signals.evidence: 'Zycus Submission to Tender- MTR Q114354 ... attached the tender submission in the form of 8 files/docs' — compliant CLM tender submitted on time
AU-YEUNG Lilian Wei: 'We will only review the latest submission after the tender closing.' (arm's-length/procedural)
Lilian: 'Our answer to Queries as attached' — MTR issued clarification answers to tenderers (Q114354); further clarifications 06 Jul
'EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate)' — buyer's own tender advisor favors iCertis; carried, no reversal
SF Next_Step: 'Not able to identify champion/ exec sponsor engagement' — never reversed in 18 months
Decision Maker Alex Gautier (GM Procurement & Supply Chain) has had zero direct Zycus engagement across the deal; Horizon-event invites (14 Jun/25 Mar) went unanswered
SF Next_Step: 'wants BEST price to win ... hoped we could do implementation ourself' (Lilian) — lowest-price, self-implementation dynamic
'eval will take 1.5 mths and award is most likely mid October'; close pulled in 18 Dec -> 18 Oct to match
'She agreed I could meet team next week F2F in HK if I wanted' — buyer-agreed F2F, not yet firmly scheduled

top_rationale: Live formal CLM tender (Q114354): Zycus submitted a compliant bid on time 07 Jul 2026; buyer confirmed it reviews only after tender close — the deal genuinely re-accelerated after a ~04 May 2026 internal-priorities freeze.
Structurally weak to WIN: no champion identified in 18 months ('Not able to identify champion/exec sponsor engagement', 08 Dec 2025) and Decision Maker Alex Gautier has never engaged Zycus directly.

Competitor risk is real and advisor-backed: EY, MTR's tender-support partner, has pushed iCertis since 05 Nov 2025; Zycus mitigating via the Alliance channel with no confirmed success. Sirion Labs listed but uncorroborated.

Commoditised price war: Lilian 'wants BEST price to win' and asked Zycus to self-implement (SI) — differentiation/ROI never established; the '$ estimation' MTR asked for after the May-2025 eAuction demo was never delivered.
Buyer self-rates confidence at 30% (07 Jul); award expected ~mid-October; close date pulled in to 18 Oct to match.
Split read: momentum high on live-tender motion (submission just landed), win-likelihood low on the missing champion/exec plus a competitor the buyer's advisor favors.

drivers: ⚠️ No champion in 18 months — SF Next_Step 'Not able to identify champion/exec sponsor engagement' (08 Dec 2025), never reversed; Chauncy Chow (Primary Evaluator) and Simon Fung engage but neither advocates. ► Use next week's agreed HK F2F to convert a working-level evaluator into an internal champion before scoring closes.

⚠️ Decision Maker never reached — Alex Gautier (GM Procurement & Supply Chain) has had zero direct Zycus contact across the deal; event invites went unanswered. ► Secure direct Gautier time at the HK F2F ahead of the ~mid-October award.

⚠️ Competitor favored by the buyer's own advisor — EY has pushed iCertis since 05 Nov 2025; Sirion also listed. ► Escalate the Zycus-EY Alliance relationship and put differentiated delivery/reference proof in front of the evaluation team.

⚠️ Price-led, commoditised dynamic — Lilian 'wants BEST price to win' and asked to self-implement (SI); no quantified value delivered. ► Deliver the outstanding ROI/$ business case and reframe on total value and delivery credibility, not price alone.

✅ Live, compliant tender bid — Zycus submitted the full Q114354 CLM tender on time (07 Jul 2026) and remains a shortlisted participant; the deal re-accelerated after the May freeze, award expected ~mid-October.
✅ Structured position intact — buyer running formal clarification rounds with Zycus responsive; Zycus is a live bidder, not dark.
win.focus_now: Before eval scoring closes (~mid-Oct award), use next week's agreed HK F2F to break single-threading — win a champion and direct Alex Gautier time — and counter EY's iCertis lean with delivery/ROI proof; competing on price alone loses this.
win.ceiling_applied: Shortlisted / RFP-round stage ceiling = 60 (not binding; score 37 sits below). Forecast Best Case conviction ceiling = 80 (not binding).

drivers: ✅ Major deliverable just landed — Zycus submitted the compliant CLM tender (Q114354) on time on 07 Jul 2026 (real skin in the game).
✅ Live RFP re-accelerated after the May freeze — RFP issued 09 Jun, briefing attended 16 Jun, buyer clarification rounds 03–06 Jul; process-mode active and on-track.
✅ Fresh, advancing Next Step (07 Jul) with a dated award milestone (~mid-October, ~1.5-month eval) and a buyer-agreed HK F2F next week.
✅ Close date pulled IN 18 Dec -> 18 Oct to align with the stated award timing — forward CRM motion.

⚠️ Buyer engagement is arm's-length/procedural — Lilian: 'we will only review after the tender closing'; no warmth or preference yet. ► Convert next week's agreed HK F2F into a working session with an evaluator/decision-maker to move from compliance to preference.
⚠️ Motion is process-driven, not relationship-driven — no champion pulling internally. ► Secure an internal advocate to sustain momentum through the ~1.5-month scoring window.
mom.focus_now: Lock next week's agreed HK F2F and use it to turn procedural tender participation into real advocacy — a champion plus Alex Gautier time — so momentum carries through the ~mid-October award.

best_practices.rank_reason: No champion identified in 18 months; needed to survive EY-influenced scoring

sum.headline: Zycus submitted its compliant CLM tender (Q114354) to MTR on time on 07 Jul; MTR confirmed it will assess submissions only after the tender closes.
supporting: Close date pulled in to 18 Oct 2026 to match MTR's stated ~mid-October award (eval ~1.5 months).
Risk tail: buyer is price-led ('best price to win') and EY still favors iCertis — no champion or Decision-Maker (Alex Gautier) access secured.
sum.as_of_note: As of 07 Jul 2026 (48h safety-net): the material step is the on-time tender submission; the 08 Jul buyer email was a procedural acknowledgment, not a new delta.
